
- EAA are useful as a pre-workout and simple drink to increase protein intake, when you don't want to drink whey.

Whey is better but EAA's are much easier to eat and digest. So EAA could be used as a pre-workout, since whey is just too much to drink.

Some people like to [train empty stomach](http://alphamale.co/taking-pre-workout-empty-stomach-good-or-bad/) and some are early birds who wake up really early and go straight to the gym, these guys don’t get their pre-workout meal 60-minutes prior to the workout.

EAA can surely be a better choice as an [intra workout](http://alphamale.co/intra-workout-nutrition-benefits/) supplement, it will help in fueling your workout and will also prevent muscle protein breakdown.  

From <[https://alphamale.co/whey-vs-eaa-difference/](https://alphamale.co/whey-vs-eaa-difference/)>

Ratios

If your goal is to enhance muscle protein synthesis, or build more muscle, mimicking the amino acid composition of muscle seems fairly logical. For example, human skeletal muscle contains the following EAA amounts (expressed in grams per 100 gram):

- Leucine: 6.3 grams (20%) - Prob more like 40%
- Lysine: 6.6 grams (21%)
- Isoleucine: 3.4 grams (11%)
- Valine: 4.3 grams (13%)
- Threonine: 2.9 grams (9%)
- Phenylalanine: 3.8 grams (12%)
- Methionine: 1.7 grams (5%)
- Histidine: 2.8 grams (9%)

However, from a physiological standpoint, things aren't as simple as replicating that composition we find in our own muscle.

See, when you consume amino acids, some of them get used up before they can be incorporated into skeletal muscle. In fact, that’s exactly what happens to leucine, which tends to get [rapidly oxidized by muscle as it enters the cell](https://journals.physiology.org/doi/abs/10.1152/ajplegacy.1972.223.6.1376?journalCode=ajplegacy).

Therefore, in order to make sure you’re actually getting adequate amounts of leucine—which, as you now know, is the star of the muscle-building show—your amino acid supplement really should have more than the 20% found in skeletal muscle.

Which brings me to the next discovery: 40% Leucine + Increased Valine, Isoleucine, and Lysine is best.

That’s right, you need about double the amount of leucine you’ll find in skeletal muscle, especially if you’re concerned about lean muscle and athletic recovery.

LEAAs with 40% leucine are found to be more effective in a number of clinical studies when it comes to protein synthesis and muscle building ([study](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29183324/), [study](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26081485/), [study](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/25827594/)).

From <[https://bengreenfieldfitness.com/article/supplements-articles/how-to-use-amino-acids/](https://bengreenfieldfitness.com/article/supplements-articles/how-to-use-amino-acids/)>

Addition of glutamine to essential amino acids and carbohydrate does not enhance anabolism in young human males following exercise

From <[https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17111006/](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17111006/)>

EAA, show high levels of muscle protein synthesis at low amounts.

[Maximizing Muscle Gain and Fat Loss: Evidence-Based Strategies | Alan Aragon | The Proof EP #296](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=twS-bgS-qIU&t=7187s)
